why: resolve-governances binds artifact-type skill to the ACED squad, but ACED recuses on the SDD self-spec (boolean process-Gherkin, not ACED @rubric/@trigger agent-config suites; precedent PR #130 / #191). Re-resolved that unit's chain to the SDD defaults (sdd-spec-judge / sdd-impl-judge, sdd:*-governance bars); other artifact-types (suite/spec/code) already resolved SDD-default. Recorded, not a halt.

Cold spec-judge round 1 ALIGNED false (architect FAIL). The drafted mission-graph barrier fence let a barrier fence its own un-retired RAW predecessor: predecessor never starts, barrier never RAW-satisfied, project wedges permanently. Aggravated by the fence being fold-computed rather than a stored edge, so the graph stays acyclic and cycles reports the wedge healthy. Fix attempted: pin the fence predicate to un-retired plus a per-barrier predecessor-closure carve-out.

Cold spec-judge round 1 also FAILed builder: the new formation scenario a-project-wide-structural-act-escalates carried Given 'preserves every scenario verbatim', colliding with the frozen a-coverage-preserving-split-self-clears -- one input, two opposite Thens, invisible to the title-keyed addOnly diff. Resolved WITHOUT a Clearance floor: a split is node-scoped by definition in main's own prose, so the Givens were disjoint in fact but only implicitly; pinned the disjointness in the suite text (rather than one node) plus a scenario asserting a single-node split still self-clears. No frozen scenario edited. Judge round 2 independently verified no narrowing.

Cold spec-judge round 2 ALIGNED false: the round-1 carve-out was scoped per-barrier and did not compose. Two barriers in one project returned opposite verdicts on one snapshot (a barrier's exempt predecessor was still fenced by the OTHER barrier), violating the frozen fold-is-deterministic scenario, and wedged the project if the fence won. Fix attempted: make the exemption project-global (any un-retired barrier's RAW-predecessor closure).

Cold spec-judge round 3 ALIGNED false: the project-global exemption closed the closure hole but left the barrier-vs-barrier hole open -- barriers ARE Missions, so a barrier binds the holds-back-the-other-missions Given. Judge proved NO fold rule satisfies the block's five barrier scenarios, and derived the closed-form rule R'' with an induction proof of termination. Iteration cap (3) reached without convergence.

Cap-hit presented to the owner rather than auto-accepted, with the trajectory measured: scenario count 10->14->17, contradictions 1->1->3, and rounds 2-3 each refuted the scenarios added to fix the round before -- diverging, not converging. Root cause diagnosed as a METHOD error: the fence was specified by example (17 independent assertions, no stated fold rule), and pairwise Given overlap grows quadratically, so each added scenario created new chances to contradict. Owner directive: SPLIT. Ship the two clean halves (star blast-estimate node, formation barrier call-out); revert the mission-graph fence block entirely and re-file it as issue #224 to be designed RULE-FIRST from R'' + its termination proof + the three counterexample snapshots S1/S2/S3. Op5 lands partially and honestly; the fence is the only diverging half and nothing consumes it yet.

Deliver: conductor review of the impl-producer's blast-estimate engine caught a real violation of frozen scenario 'a project-wide touch-set computes high blast' (a touch-set reaching across EVERY work area of its project must compute high). The engine keyed breadth on RAW COUNT (bucket 4+ -> high) rather than on COVERAGE, so a fully-covered 2- or 3-area project computed medium. The producer's own test masked it by seeding exactly 4 areas, passing via the count bucket without ever exercising the property the scenario states -- the test had drifted to fit the implementation instead of the contract (same failure mode as #89). Found by probing the engine at project sizes 2/3/4, not by the suite. Fixed in CODE, never in the frozen .feature: breadth = max(countScore, coverageScore) with coverage firing only on a project of >=2 work areas, which also preserves disjointness against frozen scenario 'a single peripheral work area computes low blast' in the degenerate 1-area corpus. Scenario-21 test strengthened to pin the property at several project sizes.

Impl gate: cold sdd-impl-judge returned IMPLEMENTATION_PASS true (21/21 frozen scenarios re-derived on its own independently-constructed corpora at project sizes 2/3/4/5/8/12) and recommended ADVANCE, but surfaced a non-blocking defect worth folding in. readSensitivePaths wrapped BOTH statSync and readFileSync in a bare catch, so a present-but-unreadable file (EACCES via chmod 000, or a directory at the path) silently returned ok:true marked:[] -- reading as 'no markings'. It did not fail the frozen malformed scenario (whose Given names 'cannot be parsed', and every parse failure did fail loud), but it violated that scenario's stated rationale and failed in the DANGEROUS direction: silently UNDER-calling blast on exactly the areas a project marked as needing care. Fixed in code: only ENOENT is benign; every other read failure (unparseable / unreadable / not-a-regular-file) fails loud with computed:null + an error naming the file. Two regression tests added (a directory at the path must fail loud; absent stays the one benign read) plus SKILL.md updated. Same doctrine as the mechanical-check rule: a check that cannot classify must escalate, never default to the safe-looking answer. 25 tests green, pnpm verify 21/21.

Conductor error, recorded for honesty: the engine was committed while the delegated impl-producer was still mid-run, so that commit captured the code and tests but NOT the matching README/SKILL.md doc reconcile -- a partial state. Caught by the impl-judge's structural read (finding 4), not by the conductor. The docs are folded into the follow-up impl-gate commit alongside the ENOENT fix, so the landed tree is coherent; no frozen artifact was affected. Lesson: await a delegated producer's completion before committing its work, rather than polling file state.

Impl gate HELD, not ratified. The engine reimplemented work-area recovery as a naive first-two-path-segments walk of a single root, instead of reusing the SHIPPED convention (touch-set-correction's pure fileToNode over discoverLayouts' declared ProjectLayout[]). Consequences against the real repo: pointed at repo root it yields 25 garbage atoms (apps/website, artifacts/adr) and cannot find sdd/mission-graph at all; pointed at .agents/specs it resolves nodes only because that tree's shape happens to be project/capability, and then measures fan-in across SPEC PROSE ONLY -- never the implementation -- so centrality does not mean what the rubric says. The star deliverable could not do its job in the field. All 21 frozen scenarios stayed green because every test builds a constructed corpus where a node's two roots collapse into one. CONDUCTOR ERROR, not the producer's: the mission brief said 'Reuse the Op2 touch-set estimator for blast auto-compute', but the delegation brief passed touch-set-correction along only as a do-not-rebuild NON-GOALS note and explicitly granted design latitude ('decide how a work area's fan-in is measured') on a convention that was already decided and shipped. User correction was decisive: work areas do NOT live under .agents/specs -- that is one of a node's TWO declared roots (a hoisted-monorepo accident), and the impl root (plugins/sdd/skills) is where build-to-learn works, so a spec-only fan-in is structurally wrong. Verified live: discoverLayouts('.') returns project sdd roots ['.agents/specs/sdd','plugins/sdd/skills'], and fileToNode maps BOTH plugins/sdd/skills/mission-graph/SKILL.md and .agents/specs/sdd/mission-graph/README.md to the same node sdd/mission-graph. Directed fix: inject ProjectLayout[] and reuse fileToNode -- the exact pattern the sibling collision-ladder already ships (it imports fileToNode + ProjectLayout from touch-set-correction). No frozen scenario changes; the contract never pinned discovery.

Second defect, surfaced only by the directive to drive the CLI against the REAL repo from repo root (it did not exist in any fixture). The engine computed repo-relative paths with full.slice(root.length + 1); join('.', x) normalizes the leading './' away, so under --root . (the CLI's DEFAULT) every path was corrupted (e.g. '.agents/specs/...' -> 'gents/specs/...') and every touch-set area came back unresolved. It passed 27/27 tests because every constructed fixture passes an ABSOLUTE tmpdir root, where the slice arithmetic happens to be correct. Identical failure class to the count-vs-coverage defect: the fixture SHAPE masked the real-tree case, so the suite structurally could not see it. Fixed with path.relative and pinned by a chdir-based regression test. Lesson recorded: a constructed-corpus suite cannot verify root/path plumbing -- that needs one real-tree exercise, which the frozen suite's own 'never the live corpus' preamble correctly forbids it from carrying.

Impl gate re-judge after the node-recovery rewire: cold sdd-impl-judge returned IMPLEMENTATION_PASS FALSE. Two findings. (1) Scenario 'a marked-sensitive work area raises the computed blast' genuinely FAILS on an independently-derived fixture inside its own Given: parseSensitivePaths anchored /^sensitive = [...]$/ to the WHOLE trimmed file, so a valid COMMENTED toml marking one work area returns computed:null. The producer's fixture had picked the one byte-shape that parses. SKILL.md's claim that the shape matches manage-spec-anchors was verified FALSE head-to-head (sibling is line-anchored and lenient). (2) STRUCTURAL: centrality is inoperative-to-inverted on the target corpus -- 56 of 62 sdd areas score fan-in 0; corpus max is 4 and is 4/4 NOISE (three copy-pasted glossary rows plus a ledger log line); sdd/spec-gate, referenced by 79 files, scores 0 and live-reports a FALSE over-called. The regex is not broken: the corpus never writes bare ids as references (it writes [gate](../spec-gate/README.md), sdd:spec-gate, real paths), so the dimension measures how often a node's NAME was used as a documentation EXAMPLE -- contradicting the node README's own 'a name is not evidence' and 'how many other areas lean on'. For a tool whose headline value is catching the under-called guess, manufacturing findings from boilerplate is worse than no tool. ROOT CAUSE across all five defects of this mission (count-vs-coverage, invented node recovery, relative-root path corruption, toml strictness, centrality recall): the frozen suite's own preamble mandates CONSTRUCTED corpora and never the live corpus, so the suite is structurally blind to the only property that determines whether this node has value -- every fixture writes bare ids, the one form real prose never uses for a real reference. Owner-directed fix: both code-only prescriptions PLUS a live-corpus smoke check in a SEPARATE test file outside the frozen suite (asserting properties not magic numbers), which is what breaks the recurrence. Frozen .feature untouched.

The cold impl-judge found the FROZEN blast-estimate.feature self-contradictory on a 1-area project: 'a single peripheral work area computes low blast' (:16) and 'a project-wide touch-set computes high blast' (:123) bind the same input and demand opposite Thens; the two are disjoint only if 'a corpus' implies >=2 work areas, which the suite never says. The engine's >=2 guard resolves it to low and documents it, which makes an ENGINE GUARD the de-facto spec. Known side effect: a barrier on a 1-area project computes low, so the node README's 'the call-out and the estimate agree by construction' claim has a hole at size 1. Reconciling it edits a frozen scenario -> Clearance hard floor -> HITL, so it was NOT self-asserted. Owner (unional) directed ROUTE-not-fix, keeping this CR code-only and Clearance-free. Filed as issue #238 for the formation Warden with three options; option 2 (state the >=2 precondition in :123's Given, promoting today's guard into the contract) is closest to shipped behavior at zero code change but is still a ratified frozen edit.

Both impl-gate blockers fixed in code (frozen .feature untouched). (1) parseSensitivePaths adopted the sibling manage-spec-anchors' line-anchored regex, so a realistic commented/multi-key toml parses; fail-loud preserved (no sensitive array still throws) and ENOENT-only-benign untouched. SKILL.md's previously-FALSE 'same shape as manage-spec-anchors' claim is now true, verified head-to-head. Scenario 4's fixture rewritten to a realistic file so it exercises its own Given. (2) Fan-in matcher widened to the forms the corpus ACTUALLY uses -- bare id, project:capability skill-ref, path under any declared root at ANY DEPTH, and same-project relative sibling link -- all derived from the declared layout roots, not hardcoded; still mention-based, no SSA. Live corpus: zero-fan-in areas 56/62 -> 8/62 (10% -> 87% non-zero), corpus max 4 -> 17, and sdd/spec-gate's FALSE over-called is gone (was computed=medium/over-called/fanIn=0; now computed=high/agrees/fanIn=10). Producer surfaced two things not in the brief: the any-depth path segment is REQUIRED (spec-gate's spec side is nested at .agents/specs/sdd/authoring/spec-gate/), and relative links must be scoped to the same project or cross-project capability-name collisions cross-credit fan-in. Producer also flagged a judgment call taken honestly: the centrality buckets had been calibrated against the BROKEN matcher (corpus max 4), so a 7+ hub tier was added -- unpinned arithmetic, no frozen scenario constrains it, all 21 still pass. Perf 229x785 -> 1x785, real run 0.17s.

Structural fix for the ROOT CAUSE of all five impl defects this mission: added plugins/sdd/skills/blast-estimate/scripts/blast-estimate.smoke.test.mts -- a live-corpus smoke check in a SEPARATE file, outside the frozen suite, bound to no frozen scenario. It runs the real repo via discoverLayouts and asserts PROPERTIES not magic numbers (hub fan-in > 0; hub > corpus median; >50% of areas non-zero), so it survives a corpus reorganization and is a tripwire rather than a restatement of today's numbers. Its necessity is EMPIRICALLY PROVEN, not argued: with the matcher mutated back to the pre-fix bare-id-only behavior, the smoke check FAILS 3 of 6 while the frozen suite passes 30/30 -- reproduced independently by the conductor, not taken from the producer's report. The frozen suite's own preamble (CONSTRUCTED corpora, never the live corpus) is correct for determinism but makes it structurally blind to the only property that decides whether this node has value. Producer mutation-tested its own first draft and caught a real flaw: the tool's own source mentions sdd/spec-gate in a comment, inflating the number it measures, so self-reference is now excluded.
